name: Iran–Argentina Memorandum of Understanding (2013)
description: On 27 January 2013, the governments of Argentina and Iran signed a Memorandum of Understanding (MOU) establishing a joint truth commission to reinvestigate the AMIA bombing of 1994. The agreement was signed by Argentine Foreign Minister Héctor Timerman and his Iranian counterpart. [Alberto Nisman](/people/alberto-nisman), the AMIA special prosecutor, argued that the MOU was designed to shield Iranian suspects — including those subject to Interpol red notices — from prosecution in exchange for oil and trade concessions, effectively undermining the criminal proceedings he had built over a decade. In January 2015, Nisman filed a 289-page charging document accusing President [Cristina Fernández de Kirchner](/people/cristina-fernandez-de-kirchner) and Foreign Minister [Jorge Taiana](/people/jorge-taiana) of treason in connection with the MOU. Nisman was found dead on 18 January 2015, the night before his planned congressional testimony. Argentine courts later determined he had been murdered.

sources.title: Argentina and Iran sign deal over 1994 Jewish centre bombing

editorial_notes: Signed Jan 27 2013. Nisman's treason charges filed Jan 14 2015. Nisman found dead Jan 18 2015. MOU ratification was later blocked by Argentine parliament.
